Title: BenLLM-Eval: A Comprehensive Evaluation into the Potentials and Pitfalls of Large Language Models on Bengali NLP
Authors: Mohsinul Kabir, Mohammed Saidul Islam, Md Tahmid Rahman Laskar, Mir Tafseer Nayeem, M Saiful Bari, Enamul Hoque
Problem: LLMs not benchmarked on Bengali NLP tasks
Task Type: Multi-task evaluation (summarization / QA / paraphrasing / NLI / text classification / sentiment)
Dataset: Custom evaluation set across 6 Bengali NLP tasks (names not clearly stated)
Main Result: Zero-shot LLMs match SOTA on a few tasks; significantly underperform on most; LLaMA-2 worst overall
Limitation: Zero-shot only; only 3 LLMs; no fine-tuned or instruction-tuned comparison

Title: An Empirical Study on the Characteristics of Bias upon Context Length Variation for Bangla
Authors: Jayanta Sadhu, Ayan Khan, Abhik Bhattacharjee, Rifat Shahriyar
Problem: Social bias in Bangla pretrained LMs unstudied; context length effect on bias measurement unexplored
Dataset: New Bangla gender bias dataset (size not clearly stated)
Method: Adapted WEAT/SEAT intrinsic bias metrics for Bangla
Main Result: Bias metrics show clear dependency on context length — overlooked in prior work
Limitation: Gender bias only; only intrinsic measures; no extrinsic downstream evaluation

Title: BanglaTLit: A Benchmark Dataset for Back-Transliteration of Romanized Bangla
Authors: Md Fahim, Fariha Tanjim Shifat, Fabiha Haider, Deeparghya Dutta Barua, MD Sakib Ul Rahman Sourove, Md Farhan Ishmam, Md Farhad Alam Bhuiyan
Problem: Romanized Bangla on internet untapped; no large-scale back-transliteration benchmark
Dataset: BanglaTLit: 42.7k samples; BanglaTLit-PT pre-training corpus: 245.7k samples
Method: Encoder-decoder with further pre-trained encoders on BanglaTLit-PT
Main Result: SOTA on romanized Bangla classification; back-transliteration feasibility demonstrated
Limitation: Informal romanized text is noisy; evaluation metric not clearly stated

Title: BhasaBodh: Bridging Bangla Dialects and Romanized Forms through Machine Translation
Authors: Md. Tofael Ahmed Bhuiyan, Md. Abdur Rahman, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum
Problem: Bangla dialects (Chittagong, Sylhet) and romanized forms underserved in MT; no benchmark existed
Dataset: New sentence-level parallel corpus for Chittagong and Sylhet dialects + romanized versions (size not clearly stated)
Method: Fine-tuned NLLB-200, mBART-50 on 7 translation tasks
Main Result: mBART-50 outperforms NLLB-200; best BLEU 87.44 on Romanized-to-Standard Bangla
Limitation: Dataset size not clearly stated; complex cross-lingual/cross-script MT remains hard; no other dialects covered

Title: BLUCK: A Benchmark Dataset for Bengali Linguistic Understanding and Cultural Knowledge
Authors: Daeen Kabir, Minhajur Rahman Chowdhury Mahim, Sheikh Shafayat, Adnan Sadik, Arian Ahmed, Eunsu Kim, Alice Oh
Problem: No benchmark to test LLM knowledge of Bengali culture / history / linguistics
Task Type: MCQ Benchmark / Cultural QA / LLM Evaluation
Method: Evaluation of 9 LLMs: GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Gemini-1.5-Pro, LLaMA-3.3-70B, DeepSeekV3
Main Result: LLMs struggle with Bengali phonetics; Bengali mid-resource; all models below English performance
Limitation: MCQ only; 23 categories may not cover all domains; no open-ended evaluation

Title: BanHate: An Up-to-Date and Fine-Grained Bangla Hate Speech Dataset
Authors: Faisal Hossain Raquib, Akm Moshiur Rahman Mazumder, Md Tahmid Hasan Fuad, Md Farhan Ishmam, Md Fahim
Problem: Existing Bangla hate speech resources outdated, binary-labeled, miss evolving implicit hate
Task Type: Hate Speech Detection (binary + 7 categories + 7 target groups)
Method: LoRA fine-tuning + prompting on LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini, others)
Main Result: LoRA boosts open-source models; GPT-4o/Gemini strong on binary but weak on implicit/fine-grained hate
Limitation: YouTube only; implicit hate remains difficult; 14-month collection window

Title: BanHateME: Understanding Hate in Bangla Memes through Detection Categorization and Target Profiling
Problem: No Bangla multimodal hate meme dataset; prior resources only binary-labeled
Dataset: BanHateME: 3,819 Bangla memes with 3-level hierarchical annotations
Method: Multimodal fusion (summation, concatenation, co-attention) + hierarchical loss
Main Result: Co-attention + hierarchical loss most effective; cross-modal alignment improves fine-grained classification
Limitation: Small dataset; cultural cue interpretation hard for models

Title: CheckSent-BN: A Bengali Multi-Task Dataset for Claim Checkworthiness and Sentiment Classification from News Headlines
Problem: No Bengali dataset for claim checkworthiness + sentiment; no joint benchmark
Dataset: CheckSent-BN: ~11,500 Bengali news headlines (LLM-annotated + manual verification)
Method: IndicBERTv2, BanglaBERT, mDeBERTa in single-task and MTL
Main Result: IndicBERTv2 achieves best overall MTL performance
Limitation: LLM-annotated labels may have noise; headlines only; single domain

Title: BanglaTalk: Towards Real-Time Speech Assistance for Bengali Regional Dialects
Problem: No real-time speech assistant for Bengali regional dialects
Method: BRDialect: IndicWav2Vec fine-tuned on 10 dialects; client-server RTP architecture
Main Result: WER reduced 12.41–33.98% vs baseline; 4.9s end-to-end delay at 24 kbps
Limitation: 4.9s latency limits conversational use; evaluated on RegSpeech12 only

Title: Reveal-Bangla: A Dataset for Cross-Lingual Multi-Step Reasoning Evaluation
Problem: Multi-step reasoning in Bangla unevaluated; unclear if models exploit Bangla reasoning chains
Dataset: Reveal-Bangla: manually translated from English Reveal dataset (size not clearly stated)
Method: Controlled evaluation of English-centric and Bangla-centric multilingual SLMs
Main Result: Models struggle with Bangla reasoning steps; context benefits non-binary questions; cross-lingual gap exposed
Limitation: Manually translated; only small models evaluated; no GPT-4 class model comparison
